subject: Anthropology
title: The Laetoli Footprints
description: Learn The Laetoli Footprints in one minute.

hook.text: 3.6 million years ago, two hominins walked through volcanic ash. Their footprints hardened into stone.

buildup.text: The Laetoli footprints in Tanzania are the oldest direct evidence of bipedal walking in our evolutionary lineage.

discovery.text: The prints show a modern gait: heel strike, arch support, and a forward-pushing big toe, just like ours.

twist.text: The makers had ape-sized brains. They walked like us millions of years before they thought like us.

climax.text: Laetoli captures a moment frozen in time — proof that our ancestors walked upright long before tools or language.

punchline.text: Two steps in ash changed our understanding forever.

quiz.question: What do the Laetoli footprints prove?
options: Bipedal walking existed 3.6 million years ago
Hominins lived in groups of two
